title: kernel.visual_debug.console_stream IPC (renderer console + exception stream)
goal: Per spec §6.4.3 (AC-VISUAL-DEBUGGER-IPC) implement `kernel.visual_debug.console_stream(scope)` exposing a streaming subscription to renderer console + exception events via CDP `Runtime.consoleAPICalled` + `Runtime.exceptionThrown` + Playwright `pageerror`. Consumed by the Diagnostics panel for live console viewing.
expected_diff_shape: Tauri command + event emission via tauri::Window.emit for streaming; CDP subscription logic in cdp_client.rs; test asserts events flow end-to-end.
unblock_conditions: MT-022 already implements the subscription in test scope; this MT exposes the same subscription as an IPC stream for Diagnostics panel.
implementation_notes: Per spec §6.4.3. cdp_client.rs adds `console_stream(scope, sink: ConsoleSink)` where ConsoleSink is a trait `fn on_event(event: ConsoleEvent)`. ConsoleEvent enum: `Log { level, message, timestamp }`, `Exception { message, stack, timestamp }`, `PageError { message, timestamp }`. The streaming subscription holds a websocket connection to CDP and forwards filtered events to the sink. Tauri command: `#[tauri::command] async fn visual_debug_console_stream_start(window: Window, scope: ConsoleScope) -> Result<StreamId, String>` returns a StreamId; events flow via `window.emit("console_event", payload)` (Tauri's event emission); companion `visual_debug_console_stream_stop(stream_id: StreamId)` cleans up. The Tauri event channel id is documented in MT-013 manual content. Per HBR-VIS-002 the console scan (MT-022) reuses the same subscription primitive — extract common code into cdp_client.rs and have MT-022's scan call into it. Tests: start stream, inject console.error via page.evaluate, assert event received via Tauri event listener; stop stream and assert no further events.
risk_if_missed: Without streaming IPC, Diagnostics panel can only poll for console events; HBR-VIS-001 live observation requirement degraded.
validator_focus: Stop cleanly unregisters the CDP subscription (no leak); the subscription primitive is shared with MT-022 (no duplicated code); Tauri event channel id matches manual entry.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Stop call unregisters the CDP subscription (asserted by leak test).
Subscription primitive is shared between MT-022 scan and MT-025 stream (single source).
Tauri event channel id is listed in manual entry per HBR-MAN-001.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: kernel_visual_debug_console_stream_start + _stop Tauri commands registered; CdpPageSession::console_stream holds a websocket subscription decoding Runtime.consoleAPICalled + Runtime.exceptionThrown via shared decode_console_stream_event; stream_id is UUID v7; stop aborts the spawned task via JoinHandle::abort and removes the registry entry; events emit to the Tauri window via window.emit(CONSOLE_EVENT_CHANNEL).

integration_validator_review_prior.verdict_reason: Console-stream IPC (Tauri command + window.emit subscription + CDP wiring) does not exist; Diagnostics panel has no live console source.
